affirmations.title: Affirmations
aiChat.response.ayuda1:
I can help you with:
- Brainstorming
- Organizing thoughts
- Quick notes
- Simple reminders

Write whatever you need!
aiChat.response.ayuda2:
I'm a simulated chat, but I can be useful for:
- Writing drafts
- Making lists
- Reflecting on ideas
aiChat.response.codigo1:
Programming tips:
1. Write clean, readable code
2. Use version control
3. Write unit tests
4. Document what matters
5. Refactor regularly
aiChat.response.codigo2:
Best practices:
- KISS: Keep it simple
- DRY: Don't repeat yourself
- SOLID: Design principles
- Code review: Learn from others
aiChat.response.default1: Interesting! Tell me more about that.
aiChat.response.default2: I see. How else can I help you with that topic?
aiChat.response.default3: Good question! Although I'm a simulated AI, I can help you explore that idea.
aiChat.response.default4: That's a fascinating topic. Want to dive deeper?
aiChat.response.default5: AI simulated response: I've processed your message. How else can I assist you?
aiChat.response.default6: Noted. Do you have more related ideas?
aiChat.response.gracias1: You're welcome! I'm here whenever you need me.
aiChat.response.gracias2: Happy to help! Anything else, I'm here.
aiChat.response.gracias3: No problem! Keep exploring your ideas.
aiChat.response.hola1: Hi! I'm your simulated AI assistant. I can help you organize your thoughts.
aiChat.response.hola2: Hello! Although I'm a simulated AI, I can be useful for brainstorming.
aiChat.response.hola3: Hello! I'm here to help. What's on your mind?
aiChat.response.ideas1:
Some techniques for generating ideas:
1. Free brainstorming
2. Mind mapping
3. Ask 'Why?' 5 times
4. Reverse the problem
5. Combine random concepts
aiChat.response.ideas2:
For new ideas, try:
- Writing without judging
- Changing your environment
- Looking for inspiration in other fields
aiChat.response.productividad1:
Productivity tips:
1. Use the Pomodoro technique
2. Prioritize with the Eisenhower matrix
3. Block time for deep work
4. Review and adjust your goals weekly
aiChat.response.productividad2:
To be more productive:
- Define 3 key tasks per day
- Minimize distractions
- Take regular breaks
aiChat.response.proyecto1:
To plan a project:
1. Define the final goal
2. Break into small tasks
3. Set deadlines
4. Identify dependencies
5. Review progress regularly
aiChat.response.proyecto2:
Structure your project:
- Vision and objectives
- Main milestones
- Weekly tasks
- Success metrics
aiChat.simulatedDescription: Ask questions, brainstorm ideas, or take conversational notes
aiChat.title: AI Chat

mcpDocs.claudeCode.instructions: You can register the MCP server using the CLI command or by creating a .mcp.json file in the project root.
mcpDocs.claudeCode.note: With the server registered, Claude Code can use tools like mcp__stacklume__add_widget directly in its responses.
mcpDocs.claudeCode.optionB: Option B — .mcp.json file in the project root
mcpDocs.claudeDesktop.instructions: Open the Claude Desktop configuration file and add the mcpServers block (or add the entry if the key already exists).
mcpDocs.claudeDesktop.restart: Restart Claude Desktop after saving the file. The tools icon will appear in the conversation bar when the server responds correctly.
mcpDocs.continue.instructions: VS Code / JetBrains extension for code assistance with MCP support. Edit the Continue config.json file.
mcpDocs.continue.step1: Open the Continue panel in VS Code
mcpDocs.continue.step3: Click Add MCP Server and choose HTTP type
mcpDocs.continue.step4: Fill in the URL and the authorization header
mcpDocs.cursor.instructions: Two ways to configure MCP in Cursor: project file or global configuration.
mcpDocs.description: Configuration instructions for each MCP-compatible tool.
mcpDocs.generic.description: The server implements Streamable HTTP (MCP 2024-11-05) over JSON-RPC 2.0. Compatible with any client that supports this transport.
mcpDocs.generic.title: Any MCP client / curl
mcpDocs.noApiKeyWarning: ⚠️ Generate an API key in Settings before using these instructions.
mcpDocs.title: Connect Stacklume MCP Server
mcpDocs.vscode.instructions: Cline (formerly Claude Dev) and RooCode support MCP. Configure from the extension side panel or via settings.json.
mcpDocs.vscode.nativeDescription: VS Code 1.99+ includes experimental MCP support. Add to .vscode/mcp.json in the workspace or in user settings:
mcpDocs.vscode.nativeTitle: Native VS Code (v1.99+ with experimental MCP support)
mcpDocs.vscode.step1: Open the Cline side panel in VS Code
mcpDocs.vscode.step2: Click the MCP Servers icon (plug) in the top bar
mcpDocs.vscode.step5: In the cline_mcp_settings.json file that opens, add the header
mcpDocs.windsurf.instructions: Windsurf uses the mcp_config.json file in its global configuration directory.
mcpDocs.windsurf.step1: Open Windsurf → Settings → Cascade → MCP Servers
mcpDocs.windsurf.step2: Click the edit icon to open mcp_config.json
mcpDocs.windsurf.step3: Paste the configuration above and save
mcpDocs.windsurf.step4: Restart Windsurf so Cascade detects the server

onboarding.editModeDescription: Activate edit mode to freely reorganize and resize widgets on the grid. Drag, resize, and lock positions. Changes are saved automatically.
onboarding.linksDescription: Save any URL with one click. Stacklume automatically detects content type — YouTube, GitHub, Spotify, Steam — and extracts title, image, and metadata.
onboarding.readyDescription: You have everything you need. You can return to this tutorial anytime from Settings → Reset tutorial. Enjoy Stacklume!
onboarding.searchDescription: Use Ctrl+K (or Cmd+K on Mac) to open quick search. Find any link, widget, or category instantly without moving your hands from the keyboard.
onboarding.settingsDescription: 13 visual themes (dark and light), AI integration via MCP for Claude and Cursor, automatic backups, project management, and much more. All from Settings.
onboarding.viewModesDescription: From Settings → View mode you can switch between Bento (free grid), Kanban (workflow columns), and List (detailed view with filters and sorting).
onboarding.viewModesTitle: Three ways to view your space
onboarding.welcomeDescription: Your personal control panel for organizing links, tools, and resources. We'll guide you through the essentials in less than a minute.
onboarding.widgetsDescription: Over 120 types available: notes, clocks, developer tools, SVG graphics... and custom widgets created by AI through MCP integration.
